payload.text:
إسم videos

الوصف (It is an application that helps you to record your daily topics of interest) مع هذه المعلومات يمكن أن يكون التطبيق مصدر دخل editors الفيديوهات سواء الطويلة أو القصيرة.

أولاً: المزايا الأساسية

1. الصفحة الرئيسية:

عرض فيديوهات طويلة وقصيرة مماثل لتطبيق TikTok.

عرض إحصائيات حقيقية مثل: عدد المشاهدات، عدد المشاركات، عدد الإعجابات، عدد التعليقات، واللوحات رصيد النقاط.

فلترة الفيديوهات حسب (طويلة / قصير / النوع /المستخدمين).

1.ملف المستخدم:

صفحة شخصية للمستخدم تشمل الاسم الكامل، صورة الشخصية، @اسم المستخدم، الجنس، تاريخ الميلاد، الموقع الجغرافي، البريد الإلكتروني رصيد النقاط،

2.صفحة ملف عامة لأي مستخدم يمكن تصفحها.

3. إنشاء وتسجيل الدخول:

إنشاء حساب يحتوي على: الاسم الكامل، اسم المستخدم (@)، كلمة المرور، الجنس، تاريخ الميلاد، الموقع الجغرافي، البريد الإلكتروني، وصورة شخصية رصيد النقاط.

إرسال تحقق على البريد الإلكتروني.

التحقق من توفر اسم المستخدم والبريد الإلكتروني قبل القبول.

تسجيل الدخول عبر البريد أو اسم المستخدم + كلمة المرور.

4. المحتوى التفاعلي:

قسم الفيديوهات: رفع فيديوهات قصيرة والطويلة، تثبيت، حذف، عرض الفيديوهات للمستخدمين.

قسم التعليقات لكل فيديو.

قسم دردشة خاصة لتواصل وتعاون مع editors من أجل إنتاج أو تحرير الفيديوهات.

5. الدردشة:

دردشة خاصة بين المستخدمين.

قسم التعليقات لكل فيديو.

مشاركة الموقع الجغرافي.

إرسال صوتيات.


ثانيًا: الميزات الإضافية

6.نظام النقاط:
نظام نقاط يكافئ المتفاعل (رفع فيديو، تقييم، إعجابات بكثرة، تعليقات بكثرة، مشاركة التطبيق بكثرة،).

7. نظام الاشتراكات والمحافظ الرقمية:

كل مستخدم يمكنه نشر 5 فيديوهات مجانًا، ثم عليه الاشتراك.

الاشتراكات تتم فقط عبر العملات الرقمية التالية:


العملة الرقمية	عنوان المحفظة
Solana	F2UJS1wNzsfcQTknPsxBk7B25qWbU9JtiRW1eRgdwLJY

Ethereum	0xC5BC11e19D3De81a1365259A99AF4D88c62a8C50

Monad	0xC5BC11e19D3De81a1365259A99AF4D88c62a8C50

Base	0xC5BC11e19D3De81a1365259A99AF4D88c62a8C50

Polygon	0xC5BC11e19D3De81a1365259A99AF4D88c62a8C50

Sui	0x41629e22deff6965100a4c28567dea45036d0360e6126a9c7f9c8fb1860a36c4

Bitcoin	bc1q9s855ehn959s5t2g6kjt9q7pt5t55n9gq7gpd7


(يجب على جميع المستخدمين دفع الاشتراكات أو الميزات المدفوعة من خلال هذه المحافظ)

9. صفحة مساعدة باستخدام AI

صفحة مخصصة للدعم الفني.

يقدم المساعدة AI

في حال لم تُحل المشكلة، يتم إرسالها تلقائيًا إلى هذا البريد:↙️

jikob67@gmail.com


روابط الدعم:

الموقع 1
https://jacobalcadiapps.wordpress.com
الموقع 2
https://jacobalcadiapps.blogspot.com



10. التقييم والمراجعات:

إمكانية تقييم المستخدمين بعد كل عملية.

إمكانية الإبلاغ عن أي إساءة.

نظام حظر وتصفية للمخالفين.

11. الإعدادات والتخصيص:

تغيير اللغة (تعدد لغات).

الوضع الداكن والفاتح.

إعداد العملة المحلية.

ثالثًا: عناصر واجهة الاستخدام (UI)

الجزء العلوي:

إشعارات.

زر تغيير المظهر.

زر بحث عن مستخدمين و (فيديوهات.)

واجهة سلسة تتناسب مع الجميع.

تسجيل الدخول عبر البريد أو اسم المستخدم + كلمة المرور.

**Refining App Scope**

I'm currently honing in on the core functionality. Initial ideas focused on a comprehensive video platform. Now, I'm streamlining the app's scope, emphasizing daily topic-based video creation and revenue generation for video editors. The focus is on a niche application.


**Developing Functionality Details**

I've been meticulously sketching out the specifics of core app features. The home feed will adopt a TikTok-like experience, coupled with real-time analytics. User profiles will encompass standard personal data, alongside a gamification points system. Authentication will include email verification. Content upload will support both short and long-form videos. I'm also planning in-app chat with editors and gamification with points awarded for user interactions. The monetization strategy now includes a subscription model via several crypto wallets. AI support, primarily for help and troubleshooting, is also being incorporated.


**Finalizing UI & Tech**

I'm now zeroing in on the finer points of the user interface. I'm incorporating user rating features, reporting, and blocking capabilities. Additionally, settings will allow users to customize language, theme, and currency. The UI will follow a responsive design with a top bar for notifications, theme toggling, and search. The tech stack is finalized: React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React icons, and the Gemini API for AI help. The architecture will have a simple structure focused on a single-page app concept.


**Outlining Demo Scope**

I'm now determining what features to include for the frontend demo. I'm focusing on a video player using HTML5 with sample content, mocking the backend with local storage for data and using Google's GenAI for support chat, but without real blockchain integration. I'll mock voice and location functionalities.


**Detailing AI Integration**

I'm currently working to integrate the AI support bot, aiming for a streamlined experience. I'll use the 'gemini-2.5-flash' model for handling basic user queries. My current plan is to build a service for the Gemini API and integrate it into the `SupportChat` UI component. The main focus is to ensure the bot responds to common questions effectively.


**Incorporating Arabic Details**

I'm now integrating the Arabic specifics. The UI will remain in English for a standard codebase, but I'll implement a toggle for localized strings. The Arabic description and wallet addresses will be exactly as provided. Additionally, I'll update the AI support to include a system prompt for the Gemini model and a fallback email. The "Language" toggle in settings will also be added and mocked.


**Organizing Project Structure**

I'm presently building out the necessary files and defining component structure. `App.tsx` will serve as the main controller. Smaller components will be either merged into it or created as distinct files for better maintainability. I'm focusing on balancing modularity with the 'handful of files' constraint. I'm also ensuring the service and type definitions are well-organized in their respective directories. I'm ready to begin work on the feed component.


**Constructing Core UI Components**

I'm now prioritizing the UI. I've got vertical scroll snap for "Shorts" videos, but standard lists for "Long" ones. Dark mode is enabled with `bg-gray-900 text-white`. I'm prominently displaying points. I'm checking imports, Gemini API usage, wallet list accuracy, and the fallback email, which is crucial.  Also, I've got to ensure those crypto wallet addresses are correctly displayed.


**Mapping Out File Structure**

I'm focusing now on the project structure, opting for a balance of robustness and conciseness, especially considering the "handful of files" constraint. Initially, I'll group types, constants, the AI service, and the App's routing/layout in the main folder. Then, a components directory with VideoFeed, Auth, Profile, Support, Subscription, and Layout components will keep things manageable. The selected dependencies include lucide-react, framer-motion, clsx, and tailwind-merge. The 'gemini-2.5-flash' model will be used for AI support, keeping it as concise as possible.


**Initiating Support Chat**

I'm now starting on the AI support integration. The 'gemini-2.5-flash' model is set with a system prompt: help users with recording, points, subscriptions, and editing queries, and fallback to jikob67@gmail.com if they cannot be solved.

entries.description: App metadata including permissions for camera and microphone
Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS and Layout
Service to handle Gemini API calls for Support AI
Main Application Component containing Logic, Routing and Sub-components

diagnostics.message: Module '"../constants"' declares 'WalletInfo' locally, but it is not exported.

payload.text:
**Investigating Export Issues**

I'm currently trying to resolve a compilation error within `components/Subscription.tsx`. The crux of the issue revolves around an export declaration for `WalletInfo` within the `../constants` module. The error message indicates that this symbol is declared locally but not exported, which is causing a type-checking failure when importing it into my `Subscription.tsx`. My next step is to examine the `constants` file to confirm the proper export is present and that the paths are correct.


**Analyzing Import Errors**

I've discovered the root of the export issue. It appears the `WalletInfo` interface is declared in `types.ts` and correctly exported there. The problem is `constants.ts` imports `WalletInfo` from `types.ts`, but doesn't re-export it, which is the source of the import error. Therefore, `components/Subscription.tsx` should import from `../types` or `constants.ts` should re-export.


**Refining Import Statements**

I've decided to refine the import statements in `components/Subscription.tsx` for cleaner code. Previously, I considered re-exporting `WalletInfo` from `constants.ts`, but importing directly from `../types` is more straightforward. I've updated the import statements to reflect this, ensuring a direct and clear relationship between components and their dependencies. I'm now ensuring the typings line up correctly with the updated imports, as I work through.


**Resolving Import Paths**

My current thinking revolves around improving the import statements in `Subscription.tsx`. While I initially considered re-exporting `WalletInfo` from `constants.ts`, I realized a direct import from `../types` is cleaner. I've updated the import statements in `Subscription.tsx` to reflect this change. Now, I'm verifying the types align correctly with the adjusted imports to ensure smooth operation.


**Finalizing Imports and Exports**

I have now implemented the import changes to resolve the original compilation error. `components/Subscription.tsx` now imports `WalletInfo` directly from `../types`, which cleans up the code and solves the import issue. After carefully examining the relevant files and the error message, I can say with confidence that this change should fix the problem. I'm now ready to test the change.


**Optimizing Type Usage**

I'm now focusing on optimizing the use of types within `Subscription.tsx`. I noticed `WalletType` is implicitly utilized through `WalletInfo` in key sections like the key for the wallet. I'm carefully reviewing the places where `WalletType` is inferred or used, ensuring the logic remains sound with the existing structure. I'm making sure that I don't need to explicitly import or name `WalletType`.


**Updating Export Strategies**

I am now thinking about the best approach for the exports. It looks like it would be better for `Subscription.tsx` to just import `WalletInfo` from `../types`. I will update the file to reflect this. I have also examined the provided `constants.ts` and confirmed that I do not need to alter it.


**Refactoring Clipboard Logic**

I'm currently thinking about improving how my clipboard functionality works in `Subscription.tsx`.  I'm focusing on the `copyToClipboard` function to ensure it's robust and user-friendly. I'm exploring potential edge cases, and making sure the copied feedback is clear. I'll need to carefully test it across different browsers.


**Evaluating UI Improvements**

I'm now considering how to improve the user interface for displaying crypto payment options. I'm focusing on the presentation of wallet information, thinking about how to best display network names and addresses to the user to make the payment process more intuitive and visually appealing. I'm particularly evaluating the balance between clarity and aesthetic.


**Streamlining Payment Display**

I'm now thinking about how to improve the display of payment options in `Subscription.tsx`. The core focus is making sure users can clearly see wallet addresses, networks, and confirm when an address is copied. This involves refining the UI to make the information clear and easy to understand.


**Testing Import Fix**

I've tested the recent changes I made to resolve the import errors. After reviewing the changes, I'm now testing the implemented fixes to verify the issue has been completely resolved and all functionality remains intact. I'm focusing on ensuring the application compiles correctly and runs as expected after the refactor.


**Addressing API Key Compliance**

I'm presently considering the minor discrepancy in API key usage within `geminiService.ts`. While the current code safely defaults to an empty string if `process.env.API_KEY` is undefined, the strict guideline emphasizes direct use. I'm focusing on the primary import issue reported to satisfy requirements.


**Fixing Export Declarations**

I'm now implementing the import changes to address the reported export error, fixing the original compilation issue. `components/Subscription.tsx` now directly imports `WalletInfo` from `../types`, which cleans up the code and solves the import problem. After a careful review of the changes, I'm verifying the fix and confirming that the application continues to operate without any regressions. I'm focusing on validating that types are being correctly resolved.
To fix the error in `components/Subscription.tsx` where `WalletInfo` is imported from `../constants` (where it is defined but not exported) instead of `../types`, I will separate the imports.

entries.description: Fix import path for WalletInfo type which is located in types.ts, not constants.ts
